title: Accessible Reader UI, Untrusted Markup, Safe Links, and Dynamic Status · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: A preserved TypeScript feed-service trace contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, validator, Fetch, stream, SQL, transaction, Promise, lease, Express, accessibility, migration, or release defect; find the earliest invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in content-safe inclusive research reader and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Treat every title, person, summary, content, image, category, language, date, and URL as untrusted even after XML validation.
Prefer text, isolate a versioned allowlist sanitizer for justified HTML, rewrite links, block active content, and test changed policies.
Use logical headings, landmarks, lists, source and date metadata, language, descriptive links, content warnings, and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A debug TypeScript team handles case 1345 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove untrusted content safety, semantic reading, keyboard focus, status, reflow, media, and motion; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Support zoom and narrow reflow, reduced motion, non-color state, image alternatives, user-controlled media, and structured text fallback.
